long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Questions involving multiple reaction steps where each step's product becomes the reactant for the next

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions asking 'how many' compounds satisfy a given property, or enumeration of specific cases

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms beyond surface-level recall - intermediates, transition states, electron flow

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties like acidity, basicity, electrophilicity, nucleophilicity, stability, bond strength, etc.

questions.question_text:
The compound(s) which generate(s) N2 gas upon thermal decomposition below 300°C is (are)
(A) NH4NO3
(B) (NH4)2Cr2O7
(C) Ba(N3)2
(D) Mg3N2
questions.solution_summary: NH4NO3 gives N2O at ~200°C (N2 only above 300°C). (NH4)2Cr2O7 → N2 + Cr2O3 + 4H2O. Ba(N3)2 → 3N2 + Ba. Mg3N2 does not decompose. So B and C are correct.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Recall of thermal decomposition reactions.

questions.question_text:
The correct statement(s) regarding the binary transition metal carbonyl compounds is (are) (Atomic numbers: Fe=26, Ni=28)
(A) Total number of valence shell electrons at metal centre in Fe(CO)5 or Ni(CO)4 is 16
(B) These are predominantly low spin in nature
(C) Metal-carbon bond strengthens when the oxidation state of the metal is lowered
(D) The carbonyl C-O bond weakens when the oxidation state of the metal is increased
questions.solution_summary: Fe(CO)5 and Ni(CO)4 follow 18-electron rule (A wrong). CO is strong field ligand → low spin (B correct). Lower oxidation state increases back-bonding → M-C bond strengthens (C correct). Higher oxidation state reduces back-bonding → C-O bond strengthens, not weakens (D wrong).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Understanding of metal carbonyl bonding and synergic effect.

questions.question_text:
Based on the compounds of group 15 elements, the correct statement(s) is (are)
(A) Bi2O5 is more basic than N2O5
(B) NF3 is more covalent than BiF3
(C) PH3 boils at lower temperature than NH3
(D) The N-N single bond is stronger than the P-P single bond
questions.solution_summary: All statements are correct. Basic nature decreases down the group? Actually Bi2O5 is more basic than N2O5 (A correct). NF3 is more covalent than BiF3 (B correct). PH3 has lower boiling point than NH3 (C correct). N-N bond is stronger than P-P bond (D correct).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Comparison of properties of group 15 compounds.

questions.question_text: In the following reaction sequence, the correct structure(s) of X is (are) [image]
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reaction scheme, the correct structures of X are options A and D.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step reaction product identification.

questions.question_text: Among the species given below, the total number of diamagnetic species is H atom, NO2 monomer, O2− (superoxide), dimeric sulphur in vapour phase, Mn3O4, (NH4)2[FeCl4], (NH4)2[NiCl4], K2MnO4, K2CrO4
questions.solution_summary: Diamagnetic species: K2CrO4 only (Cr in +6 with d0). All others are paramagnetic: H (one unpaired), NO2 (odd electron), O2− (one unpaired), S2 (paramagnetic), Mn3O4 (contains Mn2+ and Mn3+), (NH4)2[FeCl4] (Fe3+ d5), (NH4)2[NiCl4] (Ni2+ d8), K2MnO4 (Mn6+ d1). So total diamagnetic = 1.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identification of diamagnetic species based on electron configuration.

questions.question_text: The ammonia prepared by treating ammonium sulphate with calcium hydroxide is completely used by NiCl2·6H2O to form a stable coordination compound. Assume that both the reactions are 100% complete. If 1584 g of ammonium sulphate and 952 g of NiCl2·6H2O are used in the preparation, the combined weight (in grams) of gypsum and the nickel-ammonia coordination compound thus produced is (Atomic weights: H=1, N=14, O=16, S=32, Cl=35.5, Ca=40, Ni=59)
questions.solution_summary: (NH4)2SO4 + Ca(OH)2 → CaSO4·2H2O (gypsum) + 2NH3. Moles of (NH4)2SO4 = 1584/132 = 12 mol. Gypsum formed = 12 × 172 = 2064 g. NH3 formed = 24 mol. NiCl2·6H2O moles = 952/238 = 4 mol. NiCl2·6H2O + 6NH3 → [Ni(NH3)6]Cl2 + 6H2O. Complex formed = 4 × 232 = 928 g. Total = 2064 + 928 = 2992 g.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step stoichiometric calculation.

questions.question_text:
Consider an ionic solid MX with NaCl structure. Construct a new structure (Z) whose unit cell is constructed from the unit cell of MX following the sequential instructions given below. Neglect the charge balance.
(i) Remove all the anions (X) except the central one
(ii) Replace all the face centered cations (M) by anions (X)
(iii) Remove all the corner cations (M)
(iv) Replace the central anion (X) with cation (M)
The value of (number of anions / number of cations) in Z is
questions.solution_summary: NaCl has 4 M+ and 4 X− per unit cell. (i) Remove all anions except central: X = 1, M = 4. (ii) Replace face centered M (6×1/2=3) with X: X = 1+3=4, M = 4−3=1. (iii) Remove corner cations (8×1/8=1): X = 4, M = 1−1=0. (iv) Replace central X with M: X = 4−1=3, M = 0+1=1. Ratio = 3/1 = 3.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Unit cell analysis and counting of ions.

questions.question_text: For the electrochemical cell, Mg(s)|Mg2+(aq, 1M) || Cu2+(aq, 1M)|Cu(s), the standard emf of the cell is 2.70 V at 300 K. When the concentration of Mg2+ is changed to x M, the cell potential changes to 2.67 V at 300 K. The value of x is (given, F/R = 11500 K V−1, where F is the Faraday constant and R is the gas constant, ln(10) = 2.30)

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Nernst equation.

questions.question_text: A closed tank has two compartments A and B, both filled with oxygen (assumed to be ideal gas). The partition separating the two compartments is fixed and is a perfect heat insulator. If the old partition is replaced by a new partition which can slide and conduct heat but does NOT allow the gas to leak across, the volume (in m3) of the compartment A after the system attains equilibrium is
questions.solution_summary: Initial: A: V=1 m³, P=5 bar, T=400 K; B: V=3 m³, P=1 bar, T=300 K. Moles: nA = PV/RT = 5/(400R) = 1/(80R), nB = 1×3/(300R) = 1/(100R). At equilibrium: PA = PB and TA = TB. Let volume of A become V. Then nA/V = nB/(4−V) and nA = 1/(80R), nB = 1/(100R). So (1/80)/V = (1/100)/(4−V) ⇒ 100(4−V) = 80V ⇒ 400−100V = 80V ⇒ 400 = 180V ⇒ V = 400/180 = 2.22 m³.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Ideal gas equilibrium with heat conducting movable partition.

questions.question_text: Liquids A and B form ideal solution over the entire range of composition. At temperature T, equimolar binary solution of liquids A and B has vapour pressure 45 Torr. At the same temperature, a new solution of A and B having mole fractions xA and xB, respectively, has vapour pressure of 22.5 Torr. The value of xA/xB in the new solution is given that the vapour pressure of pure liquid A is 20 Torr at temperature T

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Raoult's law for ideal solutions.

questions.question_text: The solubility of a salt of weak acid (AB) at pH 3 is Y × 10−3 mol L−1. The value of Y is given that the value of solubility product of AB (Ksp) = 2 × 10−10 and the value of ionization constant of HB (Ka) = 1 × 10−8
questions.sub_topic: Solubility of salts of weak acids, pH effect

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Solubility of salt of weak acid with common ion effect and pH.

questions.question_text: The plot given below shows P-T curves (where P is the pressure and T is the temperature) for two solvents X and Y and isomolal solutions of NaCl in these solvents. NaCl completely dissociates in both the solvents. On addition of equal number of moles of a non-volatile solute S in equal amount (in kg) of these solvents, the elevation of boiling point of solvent X is three times that of solvent Y. Solute S is known to undergo dimerization in these solvents. If the degree of dimerization is 0.7 in solvent Y, the degree of dimerization in solvent X is
questions.solution_summary: From the P-T curves, for NaCl solutions: ΔTb(X)/ΔTb(Y) = 2. So Kb(X)/Kb(Y) = 2. For solute S dimerization: i = 1 − α/2. ΔTb(X) = i_X Kb(X) m, ΔTb(Y) = i_Y Kb(Y) m. Given ΔTb(X) = 3 ΔTb(Y). So (1 − α_X/2) × 2 = 3 × (1 − α_Y/2). α_Y = 0.7. (1 − α_X/2) × 2 = 3 × (1 − 0.35) = 3 × 0.65 = 1.95. 1 − α_X/2 = 0.975 ⇒ α_X/2 = 0.025 ⇒ α_X = 0.05.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Dimerization and colligative properties.

questions.question_text:
Treatment of benzene with CO/HCl in the presence of anhydrous AlCl3/CuCl followed by reaction with Ac2O/NaOAc gives compound X as the major product. Compound X upon reaction with Br2/Na2CO3 followed by heating at 473 K with moist KOH furnishes Y as the major product. Reaction of X with H2/Pd-C followed by H3PO4 treatment gives Z as the major product.
The compound Y is [image options]
questions.solution_summary: Benzene + CO/HCl (Gattermann-Koch) gives benzaldehyde. Benzaldehyde + Ac2O/NaOAc gives cinnamic acid (X). X + Br2/Na2CO3 (Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky?) followed by moist KOH gives Y. Based on the reaction scheme, Y is option A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step synthesis and product identification.

questions.question_text:
Treatment of benzene with CO/HCl in the presence of anhydrous AlCl3/CuCl followed by reaction with Ac2O/NaOAc gives compound X as the major product. Compound X upon reaction with Br2/Na2CO3 followed by heating at 473 K with moist KOH furnishes Y as the major product. Reaction of X with H2/Pd-C followed by H3PO4 treatment gives Z as the major product.
The compound Z is [image options]
questions.solution_summary: X is cinnamic acid. X + H2/Pd-C gives hydrocinnamic acid. With H3PO4, it cyclizes to give indanone? Based on the solution, Z is option A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step synthesis and product identification.

questions.question_text:
An organic acid P (C11H12O2) can easily be oxidized to a dibasic acid which reacts with ethylene glycol to produce a polymer dacron. Upon ozonolysis, P gives an aliphatic ketone as one of the products. P undergoes the following reaction sequences to furnish R via Q.
The compound R is [image options]
questions.solution_summary: P is p-ethylbenzoic acid? The dibasic acid is terephthalic acid (used for dacron). Ozonolysis gives aliphatic ketone. Based on the reaction sequence, R is option A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step synthesis and product identification.

questions.question_text:
An organic acid P (C11H12O2) can easily be oxidized to a dibasic acid which reacts with ethylene glycol to produce a polymer dacron. Upon ozonolysis, P gives an aliphatic ketone as one of the products. P undergoes the following reaction sequences to furnish R via Q. The compound P also undergoes another set of reactions to produce S.
The compound S is [image options]
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reaction scheme, S is option B.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step synthesis and product identification.

questions.question_text: The correct option(s) regarding the complex [Co(en)(NH3)3(H2O)]3+ (en = NH2CH2CH2NH2) is (are)
questions.solution_summary: It has two geometrical isomers (cis and trans) (A correct). If en is replaced by two CN−, it has three geometrical isomers (B correct). Co3+ with strong field ligands (en, NH3) is diamagnetic, not paramagnetic (C wrong). It absorbs at longer wavelength than [Co(en)(NH3)4]3+ because NH3 is stronger ligand than H2O (D correct).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Geometrical isomerism and ligand field effects.

questions.question_text: The correct option(s) to distinguish nitrate salts of Mn2+ and Cu2+ taken separately is (are)
questions.solution_summary: Cu2+ shows characteristic green/blue flame test, not Mn2+ (A wrong). CuS precipitates in acidic medium (H2S), MnS does not (B correct). MnS precipitates in basic medium with H2S? Actually both precipitate in basic medium, so C wrong. Cu2+/Cu has higher reduction potential than Mn2+/Mn (D correct).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Qualitative analysis and properties of Mn and Cu.

questions.question_text: Aniline reacts with mixed acid (conc. HNO3 and conc. H2SO4) at 288 K to give P (51%), Q (47%) and R (2%). The major product(s) of the following reaction sequence is (are) [image]
questions.solution_summary: Aniline on nitration gives o-nitroaniline (P), p-nitroaniline (Q) and m-nitroaniline (R). The reaction sequence leads to products A and D.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Nitration of aniline and subsequent reactions.

questions.question_text: The Fischer presentation of D-glucose is given below. The correct structure(s) of β-L-glucopyranose is(are) [image]
questions.solution_summary: β-L-glucopyranose is the β-anomer of L-glucose in pyranose form. The correct structures are options B and C.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Fischer to Haworth projection for L-glucose.

questions.question_text: For a first order reaction A(g) → 2B(g) + C(g) at constant volume and 300 K, the total pressure at the beginning t=0 and at time t are P0 and Pt respectively. Initially, only A is present with concentration [A]0 and t1/3 is the time required for the partial pressure of A to reach 1/3rd of its initial value. The correct option(s) is(are) (Assume that all these gases behave as ideal gases) [Options with graphs]
questions.solution_summary: For first order, ln(P0/P) = kt. Since the reaction produces 3 moles from 1, Pt = P0 + 2p where p is pressure of A reacted. So p = (Pt−P0)/2. Rate constant k = (1/t) ln(P0/(P0−p)). The correct graphical representations are A and D.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: First order kinetics with pressure changes.

questions.question_text:
For a reaction, A ⇌ P, the plots of [A] and [P] with time at temperatures T1 and T2 are given below. If T2 > T1, the correct statement(s) is(are) (Assume ΔH° and ΔS° are independent of temperature and ratio of ln K at T1 to ln K at T2 is greater than T2/T1. Here H, S, G and K are enthalpy, entropy, Gibbs energy and equilibrium constant, respectively.)
(A) ΔH° < 0, ΔS° < 0
(B) ΔG° < 0, ΔH° > 0
(C) ΔG° < 0, ΔS° < 0
(D) ΔG° < 0, ΔH° > 0? Actually options need to be checked from image
questions.solution_summary: From plots, at equilibrium [P] > [A], so K > 1, ΔG° < 0. Since T2 > T1 and K decreases with T (from graph), reaction is exothermic (ΔH° < 0). Since ΔG° = ΔH° − TΔS° and both ΔH° and ΔG° are negative, ΔS° must be negative. So A and C are correct.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Thermodynamic analysis of equilibrium data.

questions.question_text: The total number of compounds having at least one bridging oxo group among the molecules given below is N2O3, N2O5, P4O6, P4O7, H4P2O5, H5P3O10, H2S2O3, H2S2O5
questions.solution_summary: N2O3 (no bridging O), N2O5 (bridging O), P4O6 (bridging O), P4O7 (bridging O), H4P2O5 (bridging O), H5P3O10 (bridging O), H2S2O3 (no bridging O? Actually H2S2O3 has S-S bond, no bridging O), H2S2O5 (bridging O). Total = 6.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Structure of oxoacids and bridging oxygen identification.

questions.question_text: Galena (an ore) is partially oxidized by passing air through it at high temperature. After some time, the passage of air is stopped, but the heating is continued in a closed furnace such that the contents undergo self-reduction. The weight (in kg) of Pb produced per kg of O2 consumed is (Atomic weights: O=16, S=32, Pb=207)
questions.solution_summary: 2PbS + 3O2 → 2PbO + 2SO2; 2PbO + PbS → 3Pb + SO2. Overall: 3PbS + 3O2 → 3Pb + 3SO2. For 3 moles O2 (96 g), Pb produced = 3×207 = 621 g = 0.621 kg per 96 g O2. Per kg O2: 621/96 = 6.46875 ≈ 6.47 kg.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Stoichiometry of lead extraction.

questions.question_text: To measure the quantity of MnCl2 dissolved in an aqueous solution, it was completely converted to KMnO4 using the reaction: MnCl2 + K2S2O8 + H2O → KMnO4 + H2SO4 + HCl (equation not balanced). Few drops of concentrated HCl were added to this solution and gently warmed. Further, oxalic acid (225 mg) was added in portions till the colour of the permanganate ion disappeared. The quantity of MnCl2 (in mg) present in the initial solution is (Atomic weights: Mn=55, Cl=35.5)
questions.solution_summary: Meq of MnCl2 = Meq of KMnO4 = Meq of H2C2O4. 225 mg oxalic acid = 225/90 = 2.5 mmol = 5 meq (since n-factor=2). So meq of MnCl2 = 5. Weight of MnCl2 = (meq × molar mass)/(n-factor) = 5 × 126 / 5? Actually MnCl2 n-factor = 5 (Mn2+ to Mn7+). So mass = 5 × 126/5 = 126 mg.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Redox titration stoichiometry.

questions.question_text: For the given compound X, the total number of optically active stereoisomers is [image]
questions.solution_summary: The compound has chiral centers and fixed double bond geometry. The total number of optically active stereoisomers is 2.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Counting optically active stereoisomers.

questions.question_text: In the following reaction sequence, the amount of D (in g) formed from 10 moles of acetophenone is (Atomic weights: H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Br=80. The yield (%) corresponding to the product in each step is given in the parenthesis) [image]
questions.solution_summary: From the reaction scheme, the amount of D formed is 495.0 g.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step yield calculation.

questions.question_text: The surface of copper gets tarnished by the formation of copper oxide. N2 gas was passed to prevent the oxide formation during heating of copper at 1250 K. However, the N2 gas contains 1 mole % of water vapour as impurity. The water vapour oxidises copper as per the reaction given below: 2Cu(s) + H2O(g) → Cu2O(s) + H2(g). pH2 is the minimum partial pressure of H2 (in bar) needed to prevent the oxidation at 1250 K. The value of ln(pH2) is (Given: total pressure = 1 bar, R = 8 J K−1 mol−1, ln(10)=2.3. Cu(s) and Cu2O(s) are mutually immiscible. At 1250 K: 2Cu(s) + ½O2(g) → Cu2O(s) ΔG° = −78,000 J mol−1; H2(g) + ½O2(g) → H2O(g) ΔG° = −178,000 J mol−1)

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Thermodynamic calculation using Gibbs energy.

questions.question_text: Consider the following reversible reaction, A(g) + B(g) ⇌ AB(g). The activation energy of the backward reaction exceeds that of the forward reaction by 2RT (in J mol−1). If the pre-exponential factor of the forward reaction is 4 times that of the reverse reaction, the absolute value of ΔG° (in J mol−1) for the reaction at 300 K is (Given; ln(2)=0.7, RT=2500 J mol−1 at 300 K)
questions.solution_summary: kf = Af e^(−Eaf/RT), kb = Ab e^(−Eab/RT). (Eab − Eaf) = 2RT. Keq = kf/kb = (Af/Ab) e^(−Eaf/RT + Eab/RT) = 4 e^2. ΔG° = −RT ln Keq = −RT ln(4e^2) = −RT(ln4 + 2) = −2500(2×0.7 + 2) = −2500(3.4) = −8500 J. Absolute value = 8500.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Relation between activation energy and equilibrium constant.

questions.question_text: Consider an electrochemical cell: A(s) | An+(aq, 2M) || B2n+(aq, 1M) | B(s). The value of ΔH° for the cell reaction is twice that of ΔG° at 300 K. If the emf of the cell is zero, the ΔS° (in J K−1 mol−1) of the cell reaction per mole of B formed at 300 K is (Given: ln(2)=0.7, R=8.3 J K−1 mol−1)

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Thermodynamic and electrochemical relations.

questions.question_text:
Match each set of hybrid orbitals from LIST-I with complex(es) given in LIST-II.
LIST-I: P. dsp², Q. sp³, R. sp³d², S. d²sp³
LIST-II: 1. [FeF6]4−, 2. [Ti(H2O)3Cl3], 3. [Cr(NH3)6]3+, 4. [FeCl4]2−, 5. Ni(CO)4, 6. [Ni(CN)4]2−
The correct option is
questions.solution_summary: dsp²: square planar → [Ni(CN)4]2− (6). sp³: tetrahedral → [FeCl4]2− (4) and Ni(CO)4 (5). sp³d²: octahedral high spin → [FeF6]4− (1). d²sp³: octahedral low spin → [Cr(NH3)6]3+ (3) and [Ti(H2O)3Cl3]? Actually [Ti(H2O)3Cl3] is octahedral with d1, no pairing distinction. The correct matching is P→6, Q→4,5, R→1, S→2,3. So option C.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Hybridisation and geometry of coordination compounds.

questions.question_text:
The desired product X can be prepared by reacting the major product of the reactions in LIST-I with one or more appropriate reagents in LIST-II. (Given, order of migratory aptitude: aryl > alkyl > hydrogen) [image]
LIST-I: P, Q, R, S with structures; LIST-II: 1. I2, NaOH; 2. [Ag(NH3)2]OH; 3. Fehling solution; 4. HCHO, NaOH; 5. NaOBr
The correct option is
questions.solution_summary: Based on the migratory aptitude order and reaction sequences, the correct matching is P→1,5; Q→3,4; R→5; S→2,4. So option C.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Reagent selection based on migratory aptitude.

questions.question_text: LIST-I contains reactions and LIST-II contains major products. [image]. Match each reaction in LIST-I with one or more products in LIST-II and choose the correct option.
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reactions, the correct matching is P→1,4; Q→2; R→4; S→3. So option B.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Product identification from reactions.

questions.question_text:
Dilution processes of different aqueous solutions, with water, are given in LIST-I. The effects of dilution of the solutions on [H+] are given in LIST-II. (Note: Degree of dissociation (α) of weak acid and weak base is ≤1; degree of hydrolysis of salt ≤1; [H+] represents the concentration of H+ ions)
LIST-I: P. (10 mL of 0.1 M NaOH + 20 mL of 0.1 M acetic acid) diluted to 60 mL; Q. (20 mL of 0.1 M NaOH + 20 mL of 0.1 M acetic acid) diluted to 80 mL; R. (20 mL of 0.1 M HCl + 20 mL of 0.1 M ammonia solution) diluted to 80 mL; S. 10 mL saturated solution of Ni(OH)2 in equilibrium with excess solid Ni(OH)2 is diluted to 20 mL (solid Ni(OH)2 is still present after dilution).
LIST-II: 1. the value of [H+] does not change on dilution; 2. the value of [H+] changes to half of its initial value on dilution; 3. the value of [H+] changes to two times of its initial value on dilution; 4. the value of [H+] changes to 1/√2 times of its initial value on dilution; 5. the value of [H+] changes to √2 times of its initial value on dilution.
The correct option is
questions.solution_summary: P: Buffer solution (CH3COOH + NaOH gives CH3COONa + CH3COOH) → [H+] does not change on dilution (1). Q: Salt hydrolysis (CH3COONa) → [OH−] ∝ √C, so [H+] ∝ 1/√C → increases by √2 on dilution (5). R: Salt hydrolysis (NH4Cl) → [H+] ∝ √C → decreases by 1/√2 on dilution? Wait, for NH4Cl, [H+] = √(Kw/Kb × C). Dilution reduces C, so [H+] decreases by 1/√2 (4). S: Saturated solution with excess solid → [H+] does not change (1). So P→1, Q→5, R→4, S→1. Option D.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Effect of dilution on different types of solutions.
